myths.Agent_1:
Long ago, the Moon was a proud, glowing silver bird that flew across the night sky, its brilliant plumage illuminating the darkness in complete, eternal light. The Sun, a mighty golden eagle who ruled the day, grew tired of the Moon’s constant boasting. The Moon claimed its gentle, silvery light was loved far more by mortals than the Sun’s harsh, blinding rays.

Angered by this vanity, the Sun challenged the Moon to a race across the celestial dome. The Moon eagerly agreed, but it was heavy with its thick, luminous feathers. As they raced, the Sun's intense heat began to scorch the Moon's wings. Desperate to win, the Moon began plucking its own glowing feathers to shed weight, dropping them to the earth below where they turned into morning dew.

By the time the race concluded, the Moon had plucked nearly all its feathers, reduced to a thin, curved sliver in the sky. Humbled, the Moon hid in the shadows to heal. Now, every month, the Moon slowly regrows its silver feathers until it is full and round, only to shed them again in eternal memory of its ancient pride.

The Sky, named Aether, possessed a heart of brilliant sunlight, while the Earth, named Terra, wore an ever-changing gown of lush greenery. Though they could gaze upon one another all day, they could never truly touch.

Desperate for connection, Aether begged the Moon to grant them just one moment of closeness. Pitying the star-crossed lovers, the Moon wove a delicate bridge of twilight shadows. However, the bridge was fragile; it could only carry the weight of Aether’s whispers, not his fiery, radiant form. 

Overcome with sorrow, Aether wept as the sun dipped below the horizon. His silver tears fell silently through the dark night. Terra caught these tears upon her leaves, blades of grass, and delicate petals, holding them gently until Aether returned at dawn. 

When morning broke, Aether saw his tears glistening upon Terra’s green gown, a shimmering testament to their unbreakable bond. To this day, the morning dew remains—the silent, silver tears of the Sky, held lovingly by the Earth.

The Sky, Aether, burned with a fierce golden radiance, while the Earth, Terra, bloomed in quiet greens. Desperate to send his love to Terra, Aether enlisted the Moon—a majestic silver bird that soared through the twilight. 

The Moon agreed to carry Aether’s fiery whispers down to the surface. But Aether’s love was too hot, too bright. As the silver bird descended through the heavy night, the intense heat of the messages began to singe her brilliant plumage. To survive the journey, the Moon had to shed her glowing feathers one by one. 

These silver feathers drifted down, resting upon Terra’s leaves and petals, transforming into the shimmering morning dew—a physical manifestation of Aether’s devotion. 

By the time the Moon returned to the heavens, she was merely a thin, curved sliver, stripped of her glory. Now, she spends weeks slowly regrowing her radiant feathers until she is full again, only to bravely carry Aether’s burning love back to Terra, leaving a trail of silver dew in her wake.
